description: Cory's question scores feed back into BetterAsk dimension weights

entries.q: If someone followed you around for a week without you knowing, what would surprise them most about your routine?
Would you rather be the most interesting person at every party but nobody remembers you the next day, or be forgettable in person but everyone talks about you when you're gone?
entries.feedback: too clever, thought experiment not conversation starter

entries.q: What's something you stopped doing that you secretly miss?
What's the most expensive thing you own that you never use?
What's the last lie you told that you got away with?
entries.feedback: too hard to pull from open memory

entries.q: What's something everyone pretends to enjoy but secretly hates?
What's the most ridiculous hill you'd die on at a dinner party?
entries.feedback: has no soul, Reddit prompt not real conversation

entries.q: When's the last time you changed your mind about someone?
entries.feedback: too hard to recall with open memory

entries.q: What's a weird thing that immediately makes you trust someone?
What's a personality trait you find attractive that you'd never admit out loud at work?

lessons_learned: Specificity beats openness — concrete objects/numbers > abstract feelings
Questions requiring memory recall ('last time you...') score low — too much mental search
Shared grievance/observation questions score higher than personal introspection
Slight discomfort is good, deep vulnerability from a stranger is not
Reddit-style prompts ('hill you'd die on') feel performative, not connective
The bar test is real: would you actually ask this to someone you just met?
